source.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

claim_chain: SPOP exhibits context-dependent tumor-suppressing or tumor-promoting effects in different tumor types
Somatic mutations occur widely throughout the gene, making functional annotation challenging
We performed DMS covering 96.4% of all possible single amino acid variants
Our assay reliably assigns variants as Likely LoF when they impair SPOP function
The scoring system shows robust performance for prostate cancer variants but limitations for endometrial cancer variants
This work presents a nearly complete functional atlas enabling refined interpretation of clinical impact

typical_pattern: State the biological context and complexity of the protein
Identify the gap or challenge in current understanding
Present key findings and their validation
Conclude with broader implications and future directions

likely_priorities: Validation of DMS scores against known pathogenic variants
Mechanistic relevance of yeast-based assay to human tumor biology
Coverage and completeness of variant library
Distinction between loss-of-function and gain-of-function variants
likely_attacks: Yeast-based assay may not capture all substrate interactions in human cells
Inability to distinguish neutral from gain-of-function variants limits interpretability
Weaker performance for germline mutations versus somatic mutations
Lack of native SPOP substrates in yeast raises questions about physiological relevance
Tolerated classification contains mixture of neutral and pathogenic variants
reviewer_preference_profile.inference_basis: The discussion explicitly states multiple limitations: 'Our study has several limitations. The assay system is yeast-based and may not cover all substrate interactions that occur in human cells. Yeast lacks an endogenous SPOP ortholog and does not encode recognizable homologs of major mammalian SPOP substrates.' The authors also note 'our assay also showed limitations in distinguishing neutral variants from GoF variants' and 'Tolerated calls should be interpreted with caution and may not be used as a filter for excluding potentially relevant variants.' They acknowledge 'it does not fully capture the malfunctionality of SPOP caused by germline mutations.' These self-identified limitations form the basis for inferring likely reviewer concerns.

examples.quoted_phrase: our assay reliably assigns variants as Likely LoF when they impair SPOP function in a manner consistent with loss of substrate recognition and/or degradation activity
This likely reflects that the Tolerated class contains a mixture of truly neutral variants and pathogenic variants acting through non-LoF mechanisms
our score showed robust performance when evaluated using the ClinVar-Somatic and COSMIC-PC reference set
This discrepancy may reflect distinct underlying molecular pathway in somatic vs. germline contexts
This resource may provide a foundation for future mechanistic and translational studies

verification_status.notes: All extracted phrases are literal substrings from the input text. No external references, author names, or invented numbers were included. The profile is based solely on the provided abstract, discussion, and figure legend content.
